event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: high, spend more reasoning on semantic grouping.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"codex-orchestration-docs","title":"Codex orchestration docs","color":"purple","confidence":0.89,"ids":[10000],"reason":"Focused documentation for Codex task orchestration."},{"key":"data-migration-rollback","title":"Data migration rollback","color":"red","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10002,10005],"reason":"GitHub and Jira tabs describe the same migration rollback checklist work item."},{"key":"llm-eval-datasets","title":"LLM eval dataset research","color":"green","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10004,10007,10010],"reason":"Research tabs about task or evaluation datasets, including paper and companion reference pages."},{"key":"playwright-smoke-fixture","title":"Playwright smoke test fixture","color":"yellow","confidence":0.97,"ids":[10008,10011],"reason":"GitHub and Jira tabs are the same frontend testing issue around a Playwright smoke test fixture."},{"key":"browser-extension-sidepanel","title":"Browser extension side panel work","color":"blue","confidence":0.94,"ids":[10009,10012,10013,10015,10016],"reason":"Tutorial videos and side panel lifecycle docs all support Chrome extension side-panel implementation."},{"key":"rate-limit-abuse-prevention","title":"Rate limiting and abuse prevention","color":"cyan","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10014,10017,10020],"reason":"Repeated documentation on rate limits and abuse prevention, likely for the same backend or platform protection task."},{"key":"product-essay-reading","title":"Product essay reading","color":"grey","confidence":0.85,"ids":[10018,10021],"reason":"Two long-form product essay tabs grouped as a reading thread."},{"key":"mcp-integration-docs","title":"MCP server integration docs","color":"pink","confidence":0.98,"ids":[10019,10022],"reason":"Matching documentation tabs for MCP server integration across mirrored doc sources."}],"review":[{"id":10001,"reason":"Standalone UI pattern inspiration tab; not enough evidence to place it in a stronger task group."},{"id":10003,"reason":"Sensitive billing or account page; leave for manual review."},{"id":10006,"reason":"Sensitive billing or account page; leave for manual review."},{"id":10023,"reason":"Design-oriented README layout article, but it does not confidently match the other reading or engineering groups."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing migration issues**

I’m tracking a few migration rollback issues: 10002 and 10005. Then there’s some research on LLM eval datasets, specifically 10004, 10007, and 10010. I’m noting browser extension testing issues with 10008 and 10011, possibly linking them. For the side panel implementation tutorials, it seems that 10009, 10012, 10013, 10015, and 10016 could work together. 

I might combine general design references and layout notes, but I want to avoid any catch-all categories that don’t make sense!
